name: Ford
overview: Ford is a small village (historically a hamlet, originally Winterbourne Ford) in the Laverstock and Ford parish of Wiltshire, lying at a crossing of the River Bourne about 2 miles north-north-east of Salisbury city centre and bordered by Old Sarum to the north-west. It has around 185 homes and a population of roughly 475, retaining a clearly defined identity separate from Salisbury, with a 19th-century corn mill still standing over a channel of the Bourne. Wiltshire Council designates it a small village where only infill development is normally permitted.
character: A quiet riverside enclave of mostly detached houses on the edge of Salisbury, semi-rural in feel but with the city and Old Sarum close at hand.
amenities: River Bourne and historic 19th-century corn mill
Laverstock and Ford Sports Club (football and cricket)
Adjacent to Old Sarum with its shops, eateries and English Heritage monument
No shops or pub within Ford itself; everyday shopping at Old Sarum and Salisbury

commutes.notes: About 2.5 miles into the city centre
Nearest mainline station; London Waterloo to Exeter line

prices.summary: Predominantly detached family houses, some on generous riverside plots; SP4 properties here generally sell from the mid hundreds of thousands of pounds into seven figures for the larger homes.

thingsToDo: Riverside and downland walks along the River Bourne and up to Old Sarum
Exploring Old Sarum Iron Age hillfort and castle (English Heritage)
Castle Hill Country Park and River Bourne Community Farm nearby
placesToEat: The Beehive, Old Sarum (nearby pub and restaurant)
Wide choice of pubs, cafes and restaurants in Salisbury
pros: Distinct riverside village within 2 miles of Salisbury
Strong, recently built primary schooling at Old Sarum and secondary at Laverstock
Quick access to the city, Old Sarum and the A303/A36
cons: No shop, pub or regular bus service within Ford itself
High reliance on cars (most households run two or more)
Limited housing turnover under infill-only planning policy
whoItSuits: Families and professionals wanting a quiet riverside village on the edge of Salisbury with good schools nearby, who are happy to rely on a car.

sources.label: Ford - Laverstock & Ford Communities Neighbourhood Plan
